name: Nicotinamide mononucleotide improved muscle insulin sensitivity in prediabetic women
summary: A small randomized trial provides a human metabolic-function signal for NMN, but in a narrow prediabetic postmenopausal population.
statement: In overweight or obese postmenopausal women with prediabetes, 250 mg/day nicotinamide mononucleotide for 10 weeks increased insulin-stimulated muscle glucose disposal and insulin-signaling readouts compared with placebo.
population_or_model: Overweight or obese postmenopausal women with prediabetes
quantitative_note: Twenty-five participants were randomized; external summaries report a roughly 25% improvement in insulin-stimulated glucose disposal in the NMN group.
caveats: The signal is metabolic and disease-risk-specific, not a broad healthy-aging functional endpoint.
The trial was small and limited to postmenopausal women with prediabetes.
Body composition and mitochondrial respiratory capacity did not explain the effect.
